cli

Class LexicalCommandProcessor

  • java.lang.Object
    • cli.LexicalCommandProcessor

  • public class LexicalCommandProcessor
extends java.lang.Object
    Acts as a lexical pre-processor that transforms raw, fragmented command-line arguments into a sequence of cohesive, logical tokens. This analyser scans the raw input array to identify structural boundaries, acting as a sanitised buffer for downstream parsing components.

    A core feature of this analyser is token reconstruction. It transparently re-assembles fragmented inputs, such as ["--temp", "=", "yes"], into a single "stitched" and validated token ("--temp=yes"). This ensures the command logic receives a consistent stream of data regardless of how the shell delimited the original input in the OS environment.

    The processor focuses on three primary operations:

    1. Identification: It identifies and marks the boundaries of flags and options. For example, a long flag (--platform) followed by a value or a set of values.
    2. Normalisation: It handles sanitisation to eliminate "noise" and breaks down input into semantically correct individual tokens. This allows the logic layer to digest them cleanly.
    3. Filtering: If required, it strips off unnecessary characters, such as excessive whitespace, empty quotes, or redundant delimiters (e.g., consecutive commas).
    Since:
    2 March 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

        private final java.util.List<java.lang.String> tokens

    • Constructor Detail

      • LexicalCommandProcessor

        public LexicalCommandProcessor(java.lang.String[] args)
        Constructs a new instance designed to sanitise and normalise the specified command line arguments.
        Parameters:
        args - the command line arguments

    • Method Detail

      • getTokens

        public java.util.List<java.lang.String> getTokens()
        Provides the normalised tokens for further processing.
        Returns:
        the list of normalised tokens

      • flattenArguments

        public java.lang.String flattenArguments()
        Flattens the command line arguments into a single string.
        Returns:
        the flattened string with individual tokens separated by a single whitespace

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a formatted string representation of the amalgamated tokens.

        This provides a vertical list of each token in the sequence, which is useful for diagnostic output and debugging during the tokenisation phase.

        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a string describing the current state of the token buffer, or "No tokens" if the buffer is empty

      • normalise

        private java.util.List<java.lang.String> normalise(java.lang.String[] args)
        Performs sanitisation of the specified array of arguments and returns a list of cleaned tokens to provide normalisation. It also takes care of merging tokens ending/starting with '=' or ',' into single logical units.
        Parameters:
        args - the command line arguments
        Returns:
        a list of normalised tokens

      • sweepCommas

        private static java.lang.String sweepCommas(java.lang.String input)
        Using regex to collapse multiple commas into one and removes leading/trailing commas from the merged argument. Basically, it collapses multiple commas into a single comma, removes commas that are erroneously glued to an equals sign, for example: --range=,108 is cleaned to range=108 and finally strips out leading/trailing commas.
        Parameters:
        input - the un-filtered string containing multiple commas
        Returns:
        a cleaned and formatted string
